comments.author: @RondoHunter3.14
comments.text: We dug a 40 mile long ditch through Panama. The Darien Gap is about 60 miles long. When the time and money is right mankind can and will conquer the Gap. Yeah sure lions tigers and narcos oh my. When the bulldozers, excavators, and armed security comes in the battle begins. Casting aside any concerns for the native wildlife, environment and it's people, asphalt concrete and steel can be constructed to benefit the monied interests at the expense of the wonders and natural barriers Nature has provided. We've done much to rearrange the world to our needs, not always successfully. But it can be done.

replies.author: @RondoHunter3.14
replies.text: ​@trueglobe66Opportunity for man to more effectively and efficiently take everything wanted. Of course this ignores the negative consequences to the environment. We have history that SHOULD remind us of past endeavors, but it won't.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: You raise a fascinating and difficult question. Humanity has crossed many natural barriers, but the Darién Gap is also home to extraordinary ecosystems and communities. Do you think connecting the road would bring more opportunity—or create a loss that could never be reversed?

replies.text: You said it perfectly. The Darién is not a place where anyone should go unprepared — gear, medicine, and real caution matter a lot there. Even then, it still feels like one of the most unforgiving wild corridors on Earth.

comments.author: @NomadicCorner
comments.text: The Darién Gap is more than a remote stretch of jungle. It is the only break in the Pan-American Highway, a vast wilderness of dense rainforest, swamps, and rivers that has remained largely untouched, creating one of the most challenging and mysterious regions in the Americas.

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: The Darién Gap feels like nature looked at a map and decided to leave one giant “Do Not Disturb” sign between continents. Humans have crossed oceans, climbed mountains, and reached space, yet this jungle still refuses to make things easy. Sometimes the wilderness likes reminding us who's in charge.

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: This documentary beautifully showcased the wonders of our planet and reminded me how much there is still left to explore.

comments.author: @Mark16v15
comments.text: After Trump builds his highway bypassing the roughest parts of the Darien Gap, people will be able to safely cross the gap; no more deaths.

comments.author: @Mark16v15
comments.text:
The Darien Gap hasn't met Trump.  If you are an American, the Darien Gap should be on your radar, because of Trump.

Trump is pivoting America away from Europe to our own hemisphere.  His first job is cleaning the place out of all the evil cartels, starting with Venezuela, Mexico and Canada.  Eventually he'll connect North America to South America for commercial trucking purposes.  Anticipate a nice 6-lane highway that bypasses s the Darien Gap along with gas stations and police stations and even a zoo to show off the local fauna.  Rich retiree vacationers will make the 19K-mile trek from Fairbanks, AK to Ushuaia, Argentina in their RVs.

It looks like the best way to connect the two continents would be to build a 40-mile road from Hwy 1 in Yaviza, Panama east to the inlet, followed by a 15-mile bridge to Tie, Columbia that tied in to Hwy 90.  The bridge would act as a barrier to keep animal diseases from crossing from South America to the north and vice versa.  This way bypasses the gap.  A toll would pay for the road and bridge.
